title: The One with the Boobies

jokes.text: You barge in here and you don't knock? You have no respect for privacy.
jokes.setup: Someone has walked in on Rachel and she's angry about privacy
jokes.punchline: Sets up the accidental boobies viewing that the episode centers on
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the episode's main premise. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That's a relatively open weave. I can still see... your nippular areas.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to cover herself, expecting Chandler to look away
jokes.punchline: Chandler's clinical observation about fabric and made-up word 'nippular'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - awkward situation made worse by his compulsive need to make observations. 'Nippular' is a perfect Chandler-ism.

jokes.text: That's pretty much it. But you tell it really well, sweetie.
jokes.setup: Monica hyping up Roger's patient story
jokes.punchline: Roger's completely flat delivery reveals the story has no substance
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of Monica's enthusiastic setup. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Now go away so we can talk about you.
jokes.setup: Sweet moment between Monica and Roger
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately wanting to gossip about him
jokes.explanation: Honest and direct in a way that's both shocking and relatable. Good economy.

jokes.text: Think you'll do it on his couch? I don't know. That's a little weird. It's vinyl.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Monica's relationship with therapist Roger
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical concern about vinyl furniture
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical nature coming through in an intimate context. The vinyl detail is specific and funny.

jokes.text: Could I have...? We're all out. Anybody else?
jokes.setup: Customer trying to order something
jokes.punchline: Rachel cutting them off before they can even finish, clearly still upset
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's mood affecting her work. Quick and efficient character moment.

jokes.text: No, she's still upset because I saw her boobies.
jokes.setup: Joey asking what he missed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact explanation using childish word 'boobies'
jokes.explanation: Chandler's casual delivery of embarrassing information. The word choice makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It was an accident. I wasn't across the street with a telescope.
jokes.setup: Joey asking why Chandler was seeing Rachel's breasts
jokes.punchline: Chandler's oddly specific denial involving telescope stalking
jokes.explanation: The specificity of the telescope denial is what makes this funny. Classic over-explanation.

jokes.text: These aren't her 'boobies.' These are her breasts.
jokes.setup: Discussion using childish terminology
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's attempt at maturity while still discussing the same topic
jokes.explanation: Phoebe trying to elevate the conversation while missing the point entirely.

jokes.text: Pheebs, I was hoping for more of a change.
jokes.setup: Rachel wanted to change the subject, Phoebe offered different terminology
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dry observation that this isn't really changing the subject
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response to Phoebe's obliviousness.

jokes.text: I always liked 'Bazoombas.' Gives them a Latin spin.
jokes.setup: Discussion of terminology for breasts
jokes.punchline: Ross's completely made-up linguistic analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - applying his academic nature to completely inappropriate topics. The 'Latin spin' analysis is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: 'Nice'? They were 'nice'? That's it? I mean, mittens are 'nice.'
jokes.setup: Chandler calling Rachel's breasts 'nice'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's indignation and comparison to mittens
jokes.explanation: Rachel's vanity coming through. The mittens comparison is unexpected and relatable.

jokes.text: Okay. Rock, hard place. Me.
jokes.setup: Rachel demanding better compliments about her breasts
jokes.punchline: Chandler's terse summary of his impossible situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of language. Chandler's situation summarized in four words.

jokes.text: I wouldn't wanna be there when the laughter stops.
jokes.setup: People laughing at Roger's jokes
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ominous prediction about Roger's social interactions
jokes.explanation: Ominous and specific observation that hints at Roger's personality problems.

jokes.text: Whoa, back up there, sparky.
jokes.setup: Roger starting to analyze Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive nickname for Roger
jokes.explanation: Chandler's discomfort with being analyzed. 'Sparky' is a good dismissive nickname.

jokes.text: I mean, I just met you. I don't know you from Adam. Only child, right? Parents divorced before you hit puberty.
jokes.setup: Roger claiming he doesn't know Chandler
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating uncanny analytical abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Roger contradicts himself immediately, showing why he's creepy.

jokes.text: How did you know that? It's textbook.
jokes.setup: Chandler amazed by Roger's accuracy
jokes.punchline: Roger's casual dismissal of his impressive deduction
jokes.explanation: Roger's arrogance and Chandler's vulnerability. Makes Roger more unsettling.

jokes.text: What happened to the puppet guy? Dad.
jokes.setup: Mr. Tribbiani asking about Monica's previous boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Joey's mortified one-word correction
jokes.explanation: Joey's embarrassment at his father's tactlessness. Good economy.

jokes.text: Oh, excuse me. So, Ross, how's the wife? 0 for 2, huh?
jokes.setup: Mr. Tribbiani trying to be polite
jokes.punchline: Immediately bringing up Ross's failed marriages
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Mr. Tribbiani's complete lack of social awareness. Brutal and funny.

jokes.text: Chandler, say something funny.
jokes.setup: Awkward silence after the divorce comment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's desperate attempt to break the tension
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's naive attempt to fix an unfixable moment. The silence that follows is the real joke.

jokes.text: [Chandler says nothing after being asked to be funny]
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking Chandler to say something funny
jokes.punchline: Complete silence - the guy known for jokes has nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of silence. Shows how Roger has gotten under Chandler's skin.

jokes.text: Did you know this isn't Ma?
jokes.setup: Mr. Tribbiani on the phone, Joey assuming it's his mother
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's talking to his mistress
jokes.explanation: Awkward revelation handled with typical Tribbiani tactlessness.

jokes.text: Her name's Ronni. She's a pet mortician.
jokes.setup: Learning about the mistress
jokes.punchline: The bizarre and specific profession
jokes.explanation: 'Pet mortician' is wonderfully specific and absurd. Unexpected profession makes it memorable.

jokes.text: You're having an affair, I chop garlic. It's a wacky world.
jokes.setup: Dad asking about Joey's cooking method
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact connection between infidelity and food prep
jokes.explanation: Joey's simplified worldview applied to complex emotional situation. Good contrast and economy.

jokes.text: I don't know. Then you haven't.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if he's ever been in love
jokes.punchline: Dad's definitive and blunt response
jokes.explanation: Simple truth delivered with Tribbiani directness. Sage advice in few words.

jokes.text: You're burning tomatoes. You're one to talk.
jokes.setup: Dad giving love advice while having an affair
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out the hypocrisy while ruining dinner
jokes.explanation: Nice parallel between burning food and burning relationships.

jokes.text: It's like if you woke up and found out your dad leads this double life. He's like actually some spy working for the CIA. That'd be cool. This blows!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to explain his feelings about his father's affair
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by how cool it would be if dad was a spy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - gets sidetracked by the cooler version of deception. Shows his priorities.

jokes.text: Do you think Dad cheated? I don't think so. That would involve sex.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about their parents' marriage
jokes.punchline: Ross's disturbing insight about their parents' sex life
jokes.explanation: Dark and uncomfortable truth about their parents. Good shock value.

jokes.text: Why can't parents just stay parents? Why do they have to become people?
jokes.setup: Discussion about parents having complex lives
jokes.punchline: Monica's frustrated philosophical question
jokes.explanation: Universal sentiment expressed in a clever way. The distinction between 'parents' and 'people' is insightful.

jokes.text: Why can't you stop staring at my breasts?
jokes.setup: Monica's emotional speech about parents
jokes.punchline: Rachel catching Ross staring during serious moment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that interrupts serious moment. Shows the episode's main plot thread continuing.

jokes.text: Since you saw her boobies you have to show her your pee-pee.
jokes.setup: Trying to resolve the awkward boobies situation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's childish logic and terminology
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe logic using kindergarten language for adult situation. The word 'pee-pee' is perfectly immature.

jokes.text: Come on. He's right. Tit for tat.
jokes.setup: Supporting Phoebe's suggestion
jokes.punchline: Joey's unintentional pun using 'tit' in the context
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. 'Tit for tat' is perfect in this context.

jokes.text: Well, I'm not showing you my tat!
jokes.setup: Joey's 'tit for tat' comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's continuation of the pun
jokes.explanation: Follows naturally from Joey's pun but less clever. Still works as completion of the joke.

jokes.text: What's the matter with Rog? It's a little thing. I hate that guy!
jokes.setup: Monica asking what's wrong with Roger
jokes.punchline: Ross's casual delivery of intense hatred
jokes.explanation: Ross's understated delivery of strong emotion. The contrast works well.

jokes.text: You're wrong! Why would I marry her if I thought that she was a lesbian? I don't know. Maybe you wanted your marriage to fail.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his marriage choices
jokes.punchline: Roger's brutal psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Roger being invasive and accurate again. Shows why the friends hate him - he hits too close to home.

jokes.text: Wait! Go back to that 'sibling' thing.
jokes.setup: Roger mentioning sibling issues
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately zeroing in on the Monica reference
jokes.explanation: Ross's competitive relationship with Monica showing through. Natural character reaction.

jokes.text: That's ridiculous! I don't feel guilty for her failures. You think I'm a failure?
jokes.setup: Ross denying he feels guilty about Monica's failures
jokes.punchline: Monica's hurt reaction shows she heard 'her failures'
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Roger causing damage by making people examine their relationships.

jokes.text: Isn't he good?
jokes.setup: Ross and Monica fighting because of Roger's analysis
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's oblivious admiration as relationships crumble
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe obliviousness to the chaos Roger is causing. Great timing and economy.

jokes.text: I married a lesbian to make you look good!
jokes.setup: Fighting about sibling competition
jokes.punchline: Ross's ridiculous conclusion about his marriage motivation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of sibling rivalry logic. Absurd but somehow follows emotional logic.

jokes.text: It wasn't just the Weebles, but the Weeble Play Palace and the Weeble's Cruise Ship with this lifeboat for the Weebles to wobble in.
jokes.setup: Phoebe talking about childhood toys being given away
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and specific Weeble accessories
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and the 'wobble' reference is nice wordplay. Shows Phoebe's childlike attachment to toys.

jokes.text: That's tough, tough stuff.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's emotional story about losing her toys
jokes.punchline: Roger's patronizing therapeutic response
jokes.explanation: Shows Roger's condescending nature. The repetition of 'tough' is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: Mon, easy on those cookies, okay? Remember, they're just food, they're not love.
jokes.setup: Monica eating cookies
jokes.punchline: Roger's inappropriate public analysis of her eating habits
jokes.explanation: Peak Roger awfulness. Public humiliation disguised as therapy. Perfectly cringey and quotable.

jokes.text: I hate that guy!
jokes.setup: Roger's humiliating comment about her eating
jokes.punchline: Monica finally joining the 'hate Roger' club
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to Roger's awfulness. Monica's conversion is complete.

jokes.text: Oh, look. It's the woman we ordered.
jokes.setup: Ronni arriving at the apartment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about the mistress situation
jokes.explanation: Dark and funny take on the affair situation. Treats the mistress like a delivery service.

jokes.text: Oh, no, not you. Big Joey.
jokes.setup: Joey introducing himself to Ronni
jokes.punchline: Ronni's tactless clarification that she wants the father
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment showing Ronni's lack of tact. Joey's embarrassment is palpable.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! You're so much cuter than your pictures.
jokes.setup: Ronni meeting Joey
jokes.punchline: Immediately gushing about his appearance in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Standard inappropriate mistress behavior. More awkward than funny.

jokes.text: Cheese Nip? Joey's having an embolism, but I'd go for a nip.
jokes.setup: Ronni offering snacks while Joey is mortified
jokes.punchline: Chandler's observation about Joey's condition and pun on 'nip'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'nip' and accurate observation of Joey's distress.

jokes.text: Most people, when their pets pass on, want them like they're sleeping. Occasionally you get a person who wants them in a pose. Like chasing their tail. Or jumping to catch a Frisbee.
jokes.setup: Ronni explaining her job as pet mortician
jokes.punchline: The increasingly elaborate and absurd pet taxidermy poses
jokes.explanation: Genuinely dark and absurd. The escalation from sleeping to action poses is perfectly weird.

jokes.text: Joey, if I go first, I wanna be looking for my keys.
jokes.setup: Discussion about pet taxidermy poses
jokes.punchline: Chandler applying the concept to his own death with characteristic neurosis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - takes dark concept and makes it personal and neurotic. 'Looking for my keys' is brilliantly specific and relatable.

jokes.text: Oh, you left your hair at my place. I thought you'd need it tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Ronni showing up unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre explanation about returning his hair
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific detail that suggests either toupee or very strange relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: So, who's up for a big game of Kerplunk?
jokes.setup: Incredibly awkward moment with the mistress and family
jokes.punchline: Chandler's desperate attempt to break tension with children's game
jokes.explanation: Chandler's social anxiety leading to inappropriate suggestions. Kerplunk is perfectly childish for the situation.

jokes.text: If you go to a hotel, you'll be doing stuff. I want you here so I can keep an eye on you.
jokes.setup: Dad and mistress talking about staying overnight
jokes.punchline: Joey trying to prevent his father from having sex by supervision
jokes.explanation: Joey's naive attempt to control adult behavior. His discomfort with 'doing stuff' is endearing.

jokes.text: As long as you are under my roof, you're gonna live by my rules. That means no sleeping with your girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Joey laying down the law to his father
jokes.punchline: Adult child giving parental rules to cheating parent
jokes.explanation: Perfect role reversal. Joey using parental authority on his own father in moral crisis.

jokes.text: Wow, he's strict.
jokes.setup: Joey's stern rules for his father
jokes.punchline: Ronni's casual observation about the family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Ronni's complete obliviousness to how weird the situation is makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, bunking up. If you smell s'mores, don't be alarmed.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Joey having to share sleeping space
jokes.punchline: Chandler's reference to camping/sleepover activities
jokes.explanation: Chandler making light of awkward situation with camping reference. S'mores detail is specific and funny.

jokes.text: That sounds weird not followed by, 'No, it's late.'
jokes.setup: Mr. Tribbiani saying he'll show Ronni to his room
jokes.punchline: Chandler noting how the innocent phrase sounds sexual without normal context
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on sexual euphemisms. Chandler's analysis of language patterns is clever.

jokes.text: Hey, kickie!
jokes.setup: Joey presumably kicking Chandler in bed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic greeting after being kicked
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy reaction. 'Kickie' is a good nickname for the situation.

jokes.text: I can't sleep in my underwear. Well, you're gonna.
jokes.setup: Sleeping arrangements discussion
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact acceptance of uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Shows the practical discomfort of the situation. Chandler's resigned authority is mildly funny.

jokes.text: Are they end-to-end, or tall, like pancakes?
jokes.setup: Joey talking about seeing girls on top of girls
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical question about the geometry of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - turning sexual topic into logistics question. The pancakes comparison is perfect.

jokes.text: When they wanted you in your father's business, did you cave? No. You chose the out-of-work-actor business.
jokes.setup: Chandler giving Joey encouraging advice about standing up to family pressure
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Joey chose an equally unsuccessful career path
jokes.explanation: Backhanded encouragement that's both supportive and realistic about Joey's career prospects.

jokes.text: Thanks, Chandler. Get off!
jokes.setup: Emotional moment between Joey and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to physical discomfort/boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good emotional beat followed by practical boundaries. Shows their friendship dynamics.

jokes.text: Joey said I could use your shower since Chandler's in ours. Okay. Who are you? Oh, I'm Ronni. Ronni Rapalono. The mistress?
jokes.setup: Ronni introducing herself to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt identification of Ronni's role
jokes.explanation: Rachel's direct approach to awkward situation. No beating around the bush.

jokes.text: Perfect. Fasten your seat belts, it's pee-pee time.
jokes.setup: Learning Chandler is in the shower
jokes.punchline: Rachel's determination to get revenge/equality using Phoebe's earlier suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier plot point. Rachel's mock-dramatic delivery works well.

jokes.text: Chandler Bing, it's time to see your thing.
jokes.setup: Rachel's plan for revenge viewing
jokes.punchline: Her formal announcement using childish terminology
jokes.explanation: Rachel's mock-serious tone with juvenile language. The full name usage adds formality to absurd situation.

jokes.text: What's with you? I thought it was Chandler!
jokes.setup: Rachel's plan to see Chandler naked
jokes.punchline: She accidentally walks in on Joey's father instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff. Rachel's plan backfires spectacularly with wrong person.

jokes.text: You were supposed to be in there so I'd see your thing!
jokes.setup: Rachel's mistaken identity incident
jokes.punchline: Her frustrated explanation makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Rachel digging herself deeper. The explanation makes the situation more awkward.

jokes.text: Sorry, my thing was in there with me.
jokes.setup: Rachel's frustrated outburst about seeing his 'thing'
jokes.punchline: Mr. Tribbiani's matter-of-fact response about anatomy
jokes.explanation: Mr. Tribbiani's practical response to absurd situation. Good deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: Don't you think it's just that he's so perceptive it freaked you out? No, we hate him!
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to defend Roger's behavior
jokes.punchline: The guys' immediate and emphatic rejection of her theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the group's unified hatred of Roger. Phoebe's continued defense is endearing.

jokes.text: Ma! What are you doing here? I came to give you this and this!
jokes.setup: Joey surprised by his mother's arrival
jokes.punchline: She hits him twice, presumably slapping him
jokes.explanation: Standard Italian mother physical comedy. The double hit ('this and this') is effective escalation.

jokes.text: Big ring!
jokes.setup: Getting hit by his mother wearing jewelry
jokes.punchline: Joey's surprised observation about the jewelry's impact
jokes.explanation: Joey's practical observation during physical comedy. Good economy of language.

jokes.text: Your father is no James Bond. You should've heard his stories. 'I'm sleeping over at my accountant's.' What is that? Please!
jokes.setup: Revealing she knew about the affair all along
jokes.punchline: Mr. Tribbiani's hilariously bad cover stories
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Mrs. Tribbiani's practical wisdom. The accountant excuse is hilariously transparent.

jokes.text: Now he's happy. I mean, it's nice. He has a hobby.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she tolerates the affair
jokes.punchline: Referring to adultery as a 'hobby'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. Treating adultery as a hobby is shockingly casual and perfectly delivered. Peak Friends writing.

jokes.text: Look, honey, in an ideal world, there'd be no her and your father would look like Sting.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Tribbiani explaining her practical approach to marriage
jokes.punchline: Her casual fantasy about what an ideal husband would look like
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Mrs. Tribbiani's pragmatic romanticism. Sting reference is perfectly specific.

jokes.text: Ever since that poodle-stuffer came along, he's so ashamed of himself that he's been more attentive.
jokes.setup: Explaining the unexpected benefits of the affair
jokes.punchline: Her dismissive nickname for Ronni and psychological insight
jokes.explanation: 'Poodle-stuffer' is a perfect derogatory nickname that references Ronni's profession. Shows Mrs. T's sharp wit.

jokes.text: You're 10 times prettier than she is. That's sweet. Could I take her? With this ring, no contest.
jokes.setup: Mother-son bonding over comparing her to the mistress
jokes.punchline: Turning from compliments to fighting ability with jewelry as weapon
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from emotional to physical. Mrs. Tribbiani's confidence is endearing and callback works well.

jokes.text: Intense and creepy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining what her friends think of Roger
jokes.punchline: Her blunt summary of their criticism
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's characteristic bluntness. Simple but effective delivery.

jokes.text: You know, this kind of codependent, emotionally stunted, sitting in your stupid coffeehouse, with your stupid big cups, which might as well have nipples on them.
jokes.setup: Roger analyzing the group dynamic
jokes.punchline: His increasingly hostile and absurd analysis ending with nipple comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roger meltdown. The escalation to 'nipples on them' is wonderfully absurd and hostile.

jokes.text: And you're all like, 'Oh, define me! Define me! Love me! I need love!'
jokes.setup: Roger's continued hostile analysis
jokes.punchline: His mocking impression of the group's neediness
jokes.explanation: Roger's villainous monologue peaks here. The repetition and mocking tone work well.

jokes.text: And my sister, Tina, can't see her husband. He got a restraining order. Which has nothing to do with it. I found out today.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining family problems
jokes.punchline: Random non-sequitur about sister's legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey non-sequitur. The restraining order detail is random but fits his scattered thought process.

jokes.text: Things have changed here on Walton's Mountain.
jokes.setup: Joey reflecting on his dysfunctional family situation
jokes.punchline: Reference to wholesome TV family show
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between current family chaos and wholesome TV reference. Shows Joey's media-influenced worldview.

jokes.text: If I turn into my parents, I'll be an alcoholic blond chasing men, or I'll end up like my mom!
jokes.setup: Discussion about turning into their parents
jokes.punchline: Chandler's realization that both parent options are equally bad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line revealing his family damage. The punchline structure with 'or I'll end up like my mom!' is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: I broke up with Roger. Yeah, right. No, no, really.
jokes.setup: Phoebe announcing breakup
jokes.punchline: Monica's disbelief followed by Phoebe's insistence
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the resolution. Monica's skepticism is natural given Phoebe's earlier defense.

jokes.text: In some ways, I think he is so right for me. It's just... I hate that guy!
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her conflicted feelings about Roger
jokes.punchline: Ending with the group's catchphrase about hating Roger
jokes.explanation: Perfect resolution showing Phoebe finally joining the consensus. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: Clear the tracks for the boobie payback. Next stop, Rachel Greene.
jokes.setup: Joey planning to get back at Rachel for the earlier incident
jokes.punchline: His mock-dramatic train conductor announcement
jokes.explanation: Joey's dramatic flair applied to petty revenge. The train metaphor is nicely sustained.

jokes.text: Joey, what the hell were you doing? Sorry, wrong boobies!
jokes.setup: Joey's plan for revenge viewing
jokes.punchline: He accidentally walks in on Monica instead of Rachel
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Joey's revenge plot. 'Wrong boobies!' is economical and funny. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: Hello, Joey! Hello, dear!
jokes.setup: Episode ending with Joey and his father
jokes.punchline: Overly cheerful greeting after all the family drama
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom ending - artificially cheerful after chaos. Functional but not particularly inspired.

tmdb_overview: Chandler accidentally sees Rachel topless; Rachel seeks retaliation; Phoebe's irritating boyfriend Roger annoys everyone.
